topic: China fixed-term contract non-renewal: payroll and severance execution controls for foreign employers
primary_keyword: China fixed-term contract non-renewal payroll compliance

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Defines contract term structures and renewal-related legal framework.
Sets statutory severance framework and compensation exposure for unlawful handling.
Provides baseline obligations that affect renewal and non-renewal decisions.

sources.title: Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
key_points_used: Supports social insurance contribution and offboarding obligations relevant to contract-end payroll month.
Anchors control needs for contribution and filing closure during non-renewal processing.

sources.title: Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers
key_points_used: Multinational employers need process-discipline and documentation consistency in China employment decisions.
Dispute risk is often driven by execution quality rather than policy awareness alone.

sources.title: People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
key_points_used: Supports payroll-tax operational context for final settlement handling and annual IIT considerations.
Reinforces need to classify and document payment components before final payroll run.

consensus: Foreign employers should treat fixed-term contract end decisions as controlled compliance workflows, not simple admin notices.
Payroll, HR, and manager alignment is central to reducing dispute and rework risk.
Evidence quality, timeline discipline, and communication consistency are decisive in defensibility.
uncertainties: Local adjudication and authority practice can differ on specific fact patterns and evidence thresholds.
Treatment details for some payment components and filing sequences may require city-level confirmation.
English summaries may not capture all implementing detail in Chinese-language guidance.
practical_implication_for_foreign_employ: The highest-value move is to run contract-end renewal decisions through a D-14 to D+7 SOP with legal-basis coding, document gates, and payroll release controls.
